hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in the ECL2/TM5 pocket while avoiding off-target (SSTR3/5) binding through steric differentiation.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be improved by targeting non-pharmacophore positions 1/2/5/6/11/12 to disrupt off-target interactions. Iteration 14 confirms that this is the primary failure mode, and the next iteration will focus on implementing these mutations to increase Δmargin.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing on these positions will allow us to design mutations that disrupt conserved pocket contacts in off-targets while preserving key pharmacophore residues.

These mutations are designed to break conserved interactions with off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining overall sequence diversity and avoiding disruption of the pharmacophore core (positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
